title: Why More Context Makes Your Agent Dumber and What to Do About It — Nupur Sharma, Qodo
summary: Nupur Sharma argues that simply expanding context windows does not solve the problem of agents neglecting middle sections (the U curve effect). She advocates for iterative retrieval, hierarchical summarization, and self-correction as solutions, and proposes an 80/20 split of high-reasoning and lightweight deterministic models to manage token efficiency in agent orchestration.
writeup: Nupur Sharma of Qodo makes the case that larger context windows do not fix agent quality, because models neglect the middle of long inputs, a pattern she calls the U curve effect. The talk matters for anyone building agent orchestration that assumes more tokens equals better reasoning. The proposed remedies are iterative retrieval, hierarchical summarization, and self-correction, paired with an 80/20 split between high-reasoning models and lightweight deterministic ones to manage token efficiency.
